title: Omphalea linearibracteata
intro: Omphalea linearibracteata, also known as the "tongavine," is a species of plant in the Euphorbiaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. Bioactive constituents of Omphalea linearibracteata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include decoctions and infusions, which are used to treat various health issues in traditional medicine.
taxonomy: Omphalea linearibracteata is classified in the family Euphorbiaceae. This plant belongs to the order Malpighiales, which is a group of flowering plants that includes over 16,000 species. The family Euphorbiaceae is a large and diverse group of plants that includes many species of trees, shrubs, and herbs. Omphalea linearibracteata is a species of flowering plant that is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is a member of the genus Omphalea, which contains several species of plants that are characterized by their unique leaves and inflorescences.
